title: Cryptographic keys should be robust
description: Encryption algorithms are essential for protecting sensitive information and ensuring secure communications in a variety of domains. They are used for several important reasons: Confidentiality, privacy, and intellectual property protection Security during transmission or on storage devices Data integrity, general trust, and authentication When selecting encryption algorithms, tools, or combinations, you should also consider two things: No encryption is unbreakable. The strength of an encryption algorithm is usually measured by the effort required to crack it within a reasonable time frame.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

logic.detection_conditions: Usage of weak cryptographic algorithms (DES, TripleDES) OR weak key sizes (RSA < 2048, DSA < 2048, AES.KeySize = 128)
logic.triggering_conditions: (DESCryptoServiceProvider OR TripleDESCryptoServiceProvider OR RSACryptoServiceProvider(512|1024) OR DSACryptoServiceProvider(< 2048) OR aes.KeySize = 128) AND NOT in test/demo code
logic.exclusion_conditions: Exclude test code, demo code, comments, usage of strong key sizes (RSA >= 2048, AES = 256)
logic.reasoning: Weak cryptographic keys are vulnerable to brute-force attacks. DES (56-bit) and TripleDES are deprecated. RSA keys should be at least 2048 bits. AES should use 256-bit keys for sensitive data. The rule detects instantiation of weak crypto providers and weak key size assignments.
